behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: This checks a real model-output contract (embedding length) at the model→vector-DB boundary. Unit tests can validate local parsing/config, but can miss runtime mismatch when the actual embedding provider/model is used. I rejected Workflow because the behavior text only asks about a single model-dependent output property (dimensionality), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding model for a fixed query, then assert the returned vector length equals the live index dimension read from the vector DB schema/config used by the app.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is in module handoff/query wiring (query vector -> vector DB/search layer -> ranking output), which is beyond a single unit boundary. It does not require real OS/network/subprocess by behavior text, and it does not depend on LLM/AI judgment quality. I rejected System because no explicit external contract or round-trip side-effect requirement is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a controlled local vector index with known vectors, run similarity search through the real service stack (not per-function sort), and assert returned scores are monotonically non-increasing.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on real LLM judgment about nuanced intent; deterministic stubs can hide failure by forcing canned reorderings. This is exactly model-output-quality/behavior dependence. I rejected Workflow because the behavior targets one model-dependent decision point (re-ranker output), not a multi-agentic chain consistency requirement.
behaviors.test_description: For curated nuanced queries, compare raw similarity top-K vs re-ranked output using the real LLM re-ranker and assert at least one position change occurs in expected cases.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: Whether a query is truly 'no close match' is driven by embedding semantics/model output and similarity behavior, so stubs can give false confidence by manufacturing clean separations. This goes beyond pure orchestration and depends on real AI/ML output characteristics. I rejected Workflow because behavior text requires a single final decision (empty vs noisy results), not correctness across multiple model-dependent stages with cross-step consistency assertions.
behaviors.test_description: Use real embeddings on a corpus plus out-of-domain queries expected to have no semantic neighbors; execute retrieval pipeline and assert empty results when all candidates fall below the relevance threshold.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: This is full-pipeline timing across multiple model-dependent stages (embedding + LLM re-rank) in sequence; unit/integration cannot reveal real cumulative latency behavior. It also includes real infrastructure effects, but Workflow is higher because multiple agentic steps are chained in one measured outcome.
behaviors.test_description: Run production-like end-to-end requests through the live pipeline (real embedding call + vector search + real LLM re-rank), collect latency distribution over a representative sample, and assert median < 2.0s.
